items.title: Vance heads to Pakistan for peace talks as Trump issues new threats against Iran
items.standfirst: As Vice President JD Vance is on his way to Pakistan for direct peace talks with Iran, President Trump issued new threats and demands Friday as a fragile ceasefire continues to hold. Ed O'Keefe reports from Washington.
items.content: As Vice President JD Vance is on his way to Pakistan for direct peace talks with Iran, President Trump issued new threats and demands Friday as a fragile ceasefire continues to hold. Ed O'Keefe reports from Washington.

Vice President JD Vance said he believed the negotiations will be "positive" before boarding his flight to Pakistan. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak reports.

clusters.title: Artemis II crew set for splashdown after historic journey around the moon

bullet_summary: The world found inspiration and wonder in new images of the moon from Artemis II
Reported by CBS News, NPR News, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Artemis II crew set for splashdown after historic journey around the moon
items.standfirst: The Artemis II astronauts are making their final preparations before their historic journey to the moon comes to an end when they splash down in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego. Mark Strassmann has the latest.
items.content: The Artemis II astronauts are making their final preparations before their historic journey to the moon comes to an end when they splash down in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego. Mark Strassmann has the latest.

items.title: Artemis II returning to Earth for splashdown to end historic moon mission
items.standfirst: NASA's Artemis II astronauts are set to return to Earth with a splashdown landing in the Pacific Ocean after making a high-speed reentry through the atmosphere.
items.content: NASA's Artemis II astronauts are set to return to Earth with a splashdown landing in the Pacific Ocean after making a high-speed reentry through the atmosphere.

items.title: WATCH LIVE: Artemis II astronauts return to Earth
items.standfirst: The four astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II mission are about to plunge through the atmosphere toward Earth, after a successful visit to the moon.
items.content: The four astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II mission are about to plunge through the atmosphere toward Earth, after a successful visit to the moon.

items.title: What Artemis II crew can expect from reentry, according to former NASA astronaut
items.standfirst: The Artemis II astronauts are expected to splash down on Earth on Friday. Former NASA astronaut Ron Garan joins "The Daily Report" to discuss the big test for future lunar missions.
items.content: The Artemis II astronauts are expected to splash down on Earth on Friday. Former NASA astronaut Ron Garan joins "The Daily Report" to discuss the big test for future lunar missions.

items.title: Analyzing the Artemis II mission ahead of splashdown
items.standfirst: The world found inspiration and wonder in new images of the moon from Artemis II. The crew is set to splash down on Friday. Senior national correspondent Mark Strassmann reports from the Johnson Space Center. Former NASA astronaut and engineer Danny Olivas analyzes the mission.
items.content: The world found inspiration and wonder in new images of the moon from Artemis II. The crew is set to splash down on Friday. Senior national correspondent Mark Strassmann reports from the Johnson Space Center. Former NASA astronaut and engineer Danny Olivas analyzes the mission.

items.title: NASA’s Artemis II prepares for splashdown on Earth

items.content: NASA’s Artemis II astronauts are preparing for re-entry

items.title: NASA’s Artemis Crew Set to Splashdown After Moon Voyage
items.standfirst: NASA’s Artemis II crew prepares for a splashdown after a historic trip around the moon. Laurie Leshin, former head of NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory, discusses the risk the astronauts face on their return to Earth and what comes next for the Artemis program. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: NASA’s Artemis II crew prepares for a splashdown after a historic trip around the moon. Laurie Leshin, former head of NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory, discusses the risk the astronauts face on their return to Earth and what comes next for the Artemis program. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Artemis II astronauts hurtle home from moon toward splashdown
items.standfirst: The four Artemis II astronauts, returning from the world's first crewed moon voyage in over ​half a century, hurtled back toward Earth on Friday aboard their gumdrop-shaped Orion spacecraft, headed for a splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off Southern California. The finale to NASA's celebrated 10-day mission was expected to begin with separation of Orion's crew capsule from its service module, followed by a fiery re-entry through Earth's atmosphere and a six-minute radio ​blackout before the capsule parachutes ‌into the sea.
items.content: The four Artemis II astronauts, returning from the world's first crewed moon voyage in over ​half a century, hurtled back toward Earth on Friday aboard their gumdrop-shaped Orion spacecraft, headed for a splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off Southern California. The finale to NASA's celebrated 10-day mission was expected to begin with separation of Orion's crew capsule from its service module, followed by a fiery re-entry through Earth's atmosphere and a six-minute radio ​blackout before the capsule parachutes ‌into the sea.

items.title: How and When to Watch the Artemis II Mission’s Return to Earth
items.standfirst: Splashdown for the Artemis II crew is scheduled for Friday evening, and NASA will livestream the historic homecoming.
items.content: Splashdown for the Artemis II crew is scheduled for Friday evening, and NASA will livestream the historic homecoming.

clusters.title: Inflation rises to its highest mark in nearly 2 years, fueled by Iran war
clusters.neutral_headline: Inflation rises to its highest mark in nearly 2 years, fueled by Iran war
bullet_summary: 3% in March compared to one year ago, the Labor Department reported Friday, the highest such increase since May 2024
Reported by CBS News, Financial Times, NPR News and others

items.title: Inflation rises to its highest mark in nearly 2 years, fueled by Iran war
items.standfirst: Inflation rose by 3.3% in March compared to one year ago, the Labor Department reported Friday, the highest such increase since May 2024. That rise was driven by a 21% spike in gas prices in March from the previous month. Jo Ling Kent has details.
items.content: Inflation rose by 3.3% in March compared to one year ago, the Labor Department reported Friday, the highest such increase since May 2024. That rise was driven by a 21% spike in gas prices in March from the previous month. Jo Ling Kent has details.

items.title: What's behind the higher annual inflation rate in March?
items.standfirst: The latest inflation numbers are out, and they show prices jumped last month, due to the war with Iran. CBS News senior business and tech correspondent Jo Ling Kent has more.
items.content: The latest inflation numbers are out, and they show prices jumped last month, due to the war with Iran. CBS News senior business and tech correspondent Jo Ling Kent has more.

items.title: Inflation surges to highest level in nearly 2 years as energy costs spike
items.standfirst: Consumer prices in March were up 3.3% from a year ago, the biggest annual increase in nearly two years. Higher gasoline prices tied to the war with Iran accounted for much of the surge.
items.content: Consumer prices in March were up 3.3% from a year ago, the biggest annual increase in nearly two years. Higher gasoline prices tied to the war with Iran accounted for much of the surge.(Image credit: Joe Raedle)

items.title: Consumer prices jumped 0.9% in March as Iran war sent gas prices soaring
items.standfirst: Surging gas prices pushed inflation to its highest level in two years last month as the war with Iran sent gas prices spiraling.
items.content: Surging gas prices pushed inflation to its highest level in two years last month as the war with Iran sent gas prices spiraling.

items.title: Melania Trump denies any Epstein connection, seeks end to ‘lies’
items.standfirst: First lady Melania Trump denied on Thursday that she had any relationship with Jeffrey Epstein and said she was not ​one of his victims, thrusting the Epstein matter back into the spotlight after her husband had sought to put it behind him. She denied online speculation that the disgraced financier and sex offender had introduced her to Donald Trump, saying she had met her husband at a New York City party in 1998, two years before crossing paths with Epstein at another event she attended with Trump.

clusters.title: Bessent, Fed's Powell met with bank CEOs over potent new Anthropic AI
clusters.neutral_headline: Bessent, Fed's Powell met with bank CEOs over potent new Anthropic AI
bullet_summary: Financial industry leaders met to discuss potential cyber risks posed by Anthropic's latest AI model, which has found weaknesses in every major computer operating system
Reported by CBS News, Bloomberg Technology, Guardian Business

items.title: Bessent, Fed's Powell met with bank CEOs over potent new Anthropic AI
items.standfirst: Financial industry leaders met to discuss potential cyber risks posed by Anthropic's latest AI model, which has found weaknesses in every major computer operating system.
items.content: Financial industry leaders met to discuss potential cyber risks posed by Anthropic's latest AI model, which has found weaknesses in every major computer operating system.

items.title: Cyber Security In Focus As Bank CEO's Race to DC
items.standfirst: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell summoned Wall Street leaders to give them an urgent warning: an artificial intelligence tool from Anthropic PBC marks the beginning of a new era of cybersecurity. The April 7 meeting in Washington was focused on Mythos, a new AI model that Anthropic says is so good at finding vulnerabilities in software and computer systems that it can only be released to a limited number of carefully-chosen parties. If tools like Mythos fall into the wrong hands, Anthropic says, it could provide attackers with a powerful new weapon to steal data or disrupt critical infrastructure. Bloomberg's Todd Gillespie joins to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell summoned Wall Street leaders to give them an urgent warning: an artificial intelligence tool from Anthropic PBC marks the beginning of a new era of cybersecurity. The April 7 meeting in Washington was focused on Mythos, a new AI model that Anthropic says is so good at finding vulnerabilities in software and computer systems that it can only be released to a limited number of carefully-chosen parties. If tools like Mythos fall into the wrong hands, Anthropic says, it could provide attackers with a powerful new weapon to steal data or disrupt critical infrastructure. Bloomberg's Todd Gillespie joins to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.content: The Bank of Canada and the country’s major banks and financial firms met Friday to discuss cybersecurity risks raised by Anthropic PBC’s latest artificial intelligence model.

items.title: Wall Street CEOs Summoned to Discuss Anthropic AI Risks | Bloomberg Tech 4/10/2026
items.standfirst: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde discusses concerns about cyber risks raised by Anthropic’s newest AI model. US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Powell summoned Wall Street leaders to an urgent meeting to discuss the threat. Plus, CoreWeave CEO Michael Intrator discusses deals with Anthropic and Meta as demand for compute shows no sign of slowing. And, after a record breaking trip around the moon the crew of Artemis II prepares to splash down in the Pacific ocean. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: China's Xi meets Taiwan opposition leader ahead of key summit with Trump
items.standfirst: Xi Jinping and the KMT's Cheng Li-wun agreed to pursue peace, but Taiwan's ruling party worries it will enable Beijing to undermine its democracy.
items.content: Xi Jinping and the KMT's Cheng Li-wun agreed to pursue peace, but Taiwan's ruling party worries it will enable Beijing to undermine its democracy.(Image credit: Xie Huanchi)

items.title: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi Jinping in Beijing
items.standfirst: Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade.
items.content: Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade.

items.title: Xi tells Taiwan opposition leader people on both sides of strait are Chinese in rare meeting
items.standfirst: Cheng Li-wun’s visit to Beijing has sparked controversy in Taiwan, with critics accusing her of being too close to ChinaIn a rare meeting with Taiwan’s opposition leader, China’s president, Xi Jinping, declared that people on both sides of the Taiwan Strait were Chinese and wanted peace.The meeting in Beijing between Xi and Cheng Li-wun, the chair of Taiwan’s Kuomintang (KMT), is the first such contact in a decade. The visit has sparked controversy in Taiwan, with Cheng’s critics accusing her of being too close to China, a country that many in Taiwan see as a threat. Continue reading...
items.content: Cheng Li-wun’s visit to Beijing has sparked controversy in Taiwan, with critics accusing her of being too close to ChinaIn a rare meeting with Taiwan’s opposition leader, China’s president, Xi Jinping, declared that people on both sides of the Taiwan Strait were Chinese and wanted peace.The meeting in Beijing between Xi and Cheng Li-wun, the chair of Taiwan’s Kuomintang (KMT), is the first such contact in a decade. items.title: One dead and 27 injured after bus with British passengers crashes in Canary Islands
items.standfirst: Vehicle veered into a ravine on island of La Gomera while transporting a tour group for a boat excursionA man has died and 27 people are in hospital after a bus carrying British passengers crashed in the Canary Islands, local officials have said.The incident happened at 1.15pm local time on Friday when the vehicle veered into a ravine on the GM-2 highway near the town of San Sebastián de La Gomera. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Crispin Odey drops £79m libel claim against FT over sexual misconduct allegations
clusters.neutral_headline: Crispin Odey drops £79m libel claim against FT over sexual misconduct allegations
bullet_summary: The FT had said 15 women were willing to testify in court against former hedge fund boss
Reported by Guardian World, Financial Times

items.title: Crispin Odey drops £79m libel claim against FT over sexual misconduct allegations
items.standfirst: Ex-hedge fund manager ‘forced to accept’ newspaper likely to succeed in its public interest defence, say his lawyersCrispin Odey, the former hedge fund manager, has dropped his £79m libel claim against the Financial Times over its reporting of sexual misconduct allegations against him, his lawyers have said.In 2023, the FT published several articles from 20 women alleging sexual assault and harassment against Odey, covering a period of five decades. He has previously denied the allegations against him. Continue reading...
items.content: Ex-hedge fund manager ‘forced to accept’ newspaper likely to succeed in its public interest defence, say his lawyersCrispin Odey, the former hedge fund manager, has dropped his £79m libel claim against the Financial Times over its reporting of sexual misconduct allegations against him, his lawyers have said.In 2023, the FT published several articles from 20 women alleging sexual assault and harassment against Odey, covering a period of five decades. He has previously denied the allegations against him. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Starmer says staying in NATO aligns with US interests British Prime Minister Keir Starmer argued that remaining in NATO is in the best interests of the United States, setting his stance against President Trump’s threats to withdraw from the alliance. In parallel, Starmer pressed the European side…
items.content: Starmer says staying in NATO aligns with US interests British Prime Minister Keir Starmer argued that remaining in NATO is in the best interests of the United States, setting his stance against President Trump’s threats to withdraw from the alliance. In parallel, Starmer pressed the European side…

items.title: Remaining in Nato is in best interests of US, says Keir Starmer
items.standfirst: PM pushes back after Trump’s threats to leave alliance and says European members must do more in light of Iran warKeir Starmer has said it is in the best interests of the US to stay in Nato and that Europe must do more to support the alliance in light of the war in Iran.The British prime minister, speaking at the end of a multi-stop trip around the Gulf to discuss the tentative ceasefire and options to fully reopen the commercially vital strait of Hormuz, pushed back against Donald Trump’s threats to leave the defence alliance. Continue reading...
